keynotes.text: KEY-NOTES TO THE MATERIA MEDICA by HENRY N. GUERNSEY, M.D. Presented by Médi-T STRONTIANA CARB. Violent involuntary starts of the body; tension in inner or outer parts; heat, with aversion to undress or uncover one's self. Affections of the joints of the legs. Worse.-- In the night; in the evening; from cold in general; from undressing; after lying down and rising again; from rubbing; in darkness. Better.-- From light; from a very bright light; from warmth in general; from wrapping up warmly. Remedies following:-- Bellad., Caustic., Kali carb., Pulsat., Rhus tox., Sepia, Sulphur . Antidote:-- Camphor . Copyright © Médi-T 2000
